# VantageLabs

> Computer-vision tools for aerial and video footage that run entirely in the user's browser. No upload, no server, no account. Open source under MIT.

VantageLabs ships every tool in two forms: a JavaScript implementation that runs in the browser via the Canvas API, and a Python implementation that runs as a CLI (and, increasingly, in the same browser tab via Pyodide). Privacy by default — video and image data never leaves the user's machine.

The site is built and maintained by Kenny Rogers, an engineer based in Accra, Ghana.

## Available now

- [Frame Extractor](https://vantage.lodonu.dev/frame-extractor) — pull high-resolution stills from any video file at a chosen interval. Runs entirely in-browser via HTML5 Video + Canvas API. Optional Python engine (Pyodide) computes Laplacian-variance sharpness scores per extracted frame. Supports MP4, MOV, and WebM up to 500 MB. Lets users download a single frame as JPEG or all frames as a ZIP.

## Coming soon

- [Sharpness Scorer](https://vantage.lodonu.dev/sharpness-scorer) — auto-rank extracted frames by image quality using Laplacian variance.
- [Color Segmentation](https://vantage.lodonu.dev/color-segmentation) — classify aerial imagery into vegetation, water, and built areas using HSV thresholds.
- [Object Detection](https://vantage.lodonu.dev/object-detection) — detect and count objects in aerial footage using YOLOv8 in the browser via ONNX Runtime Web.
- [Traffic Analysis](https://vantage.lodonu.dev/traffic-analysis) — count and track vehicles from aerial footage with a heatmap overlay surfacing congested lanes and intersections.
- [Aerial Mapper](https://vantage.lodonu.dev/aerial-mapper) — stitch overlapping aerial photos into orthomosaic maps.

## Tech stack

- React 19 + React Router 7 for the UI
- HTML5 Video + Canvas API for in-browser frame extraction
- JSZip (lazy-loaded) for client-side ZIP packaging
- OpenCV (Python sibling for the CLI tools)
- Pyodide for running Python in the browser
- Cloudflare Workers Assets for hosting at https://vantage.lodonu.dev

## Source code and license

- GitHub: https://github.com/Kenny-Rogers/vantage-labs
- License: MIT

## Author

Kenny Rogers, software engineer in Accra, Ghana, exploring the intersection of aerial footage and computer vision.
